result.stdout:
Generates Markdown from .NET XML documentation comments.

Usage: xmldocmd-docusaurus input output [options]

   input
      The path or name of the input assembly.
   output
      The path of the output directory.

   --source <url>
      The URL (absolute or relative) of the folder containing the source
      code of the assembly, e.g. at GitHub. (optional)
   --namespace <ns>
      The root namespace of the input assembly. (optional)
   --visibility (public|protected|internal|private)
      The minimum visibility of documented members. (default 'protected')
   --obsolete
      Generates documentation for obsolete types and members.
   --skip-unbrowsable
      Skips documentation for types that are marked with System.ComponentModel.EditorBrowsable Never.
   --skip-compiler-generated
      Skips documentation for types that are marked with System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerGenerated.
   --clean
      Deletes previously generated files that are no longer used.
   --verify
      Exits with error code 1 if changes to the file system are needed.
   --dryrun
      Executes the tool without making changes to the file system.
   --quiet
      Suppresses normal console output.
   --front-matter
      File containing the Jekyll front matter template you want in each generated page.
      The front matter can use $title argument and $rel for permalinks.
      When front matter is defined the .md extension is dropped in all generated links.
   --permalink
      Specify permalink style, 'none' or 'pretty' (default 'none').
      'pretty' permalinks do not contain file extensions, and when you select this option.
      periods have to be removed from file names, for example, 'System.Console' would have to be 'SystemConsole'.
      since the removal of the '.md' extension would make Jekyll think .Console is a file extension which doesn't work.
   --namespace-pages
      Generate separate pages for each namespace, listing types in each.
   --toc
      File containing table of contents in .yml format.
   --newline (auto|lf|crlf)
      The newline used in the output (default auto).
   --type-folders
      Type markdown will be inside the same folder has it's members.
